title: A randomized controlled trial of Kundalini yoga in mild cognitive impairment
headline: A randomized controlled trial of Kundalini yoga in mild cognitive impairment
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined focus & attention. At baseline, 81 participants had no significant baseline group differences in clinical or demographic characteristics.
keyFindings: At baseline, 81 participants had no significant baseline group differences in clinical or demographic characteristics.
limitations: KY group showed short- and long-term improvements in executive functioning as compared to MET, and broader effects on depressed mood and resilience.
Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
